arguments.task: Open official GoHighLevel/HighLevel developer documentation and official first-party pages needed to determine: product description, all documented API auth methods, API surface type and breadth, rate limits, pricing or access gating for API use, and whether an official MCP server/integration exists. Visit the auth docs, API overview/reference, rate limit docs if available, pricing pages, and any official MCP docs/page. Return the final answer as concise structured notes with the exact page URLs used as evidence.

input.code_to_execute:
queries = [
    'site:help.gohighlevel.com GoHighLevel API authentication official',
    'site:marketplace.gohighlevel.com GoHighLevel developer API official',
    'site:developers.gohighlevel.com GoHighLevel API official',
    'site:gohighlevel.com pricing API GoHighLevel official',
    'site:help.gohighlevel.com rate limit API GoHighLevel official',
    'site:gohighlevel.com MCP GoHighLevel official'
]
for q in queries:
    res, err = web_search(q)
    print('\nQUERY:', q)
    if err:
        print('ERROR:', err)
    else:
        print(res[:4000])
input.thought: Use web search helper to locate live first-party GoHighLevel developer, auth, pricing, and MCP pages after the hint URL appeared stale.

results_truncated: {"_meta": null, "content": [{"type": "text", "text": "{\"data\":{\"results\":\"\",\"stdout\":\"\\nQUERY: site:help.gohighlevel.com GoHighLevel API authentication official\\nGoHighLevel officially authenticates API requests using OAuth 2.0 or Private Integration Tokens (PIT) [1][2]. Legacy API keys are being deprecated for accounts not currently using them [3].\\n\\nFor standard OAuth 2.0 app flows, your application must exchange an authorization code for an access token via the /oauth/token endpoint [1]. For server-to-server integrations, create a Private Integration in the Developer Settings to generate a PIT [1][2].\\n\\nInclude your token in the HTTP Authorization header:\\n\\n```http\\nAuthorization: Bearer {access_token_or_pit}\\n```\\n\\nTo simplify implementation, you can use the official Python or PHP SDKs, which automate token exchange, rotation, and storage [4]. Detailed documentation is available at the HighLevel Developer Marketplace portal [3][5].\\n\\nQUERY: site:marketplace.gohighlevel.com GoHighLevel developer API official\\nGoHighLevel provides an official API platform for developers through the HighLevel Marketplace documentation, which covers various services including contacts, memberships, and email management [1][2][3]. The current standard is the v3 API, which utilizes a /v3 route prefix and AIP-compliant responses [4][5]. Authentication is handled via OAuth flows documented in the official developer portal [6]. Developers can integrate using the offici...[truncated]

raw_result.description: CRM, marketing automation, and agency platform with APIs for contacts, conversations, payments, calendars, and other business workflows.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official evidence gathered indicates OAuth 2.0 for app integrations and Private Integration Tokens (PIT) used as Bearer tokens for server-to-server access. No first-party evidence was gathered for Basic auth or API keys in the current pass.
raw_result.gating_reason: Evidence gathered indicates API access depends on plan tier, with basic API access included on paid plans and advanced API access available on higher paid tiers rather than a free self-serve tier.

api_surface.breadth: Official evidence gathered indicates a broad REST API with current v3 endpoints plus legacy versions covering contacts, memberships, email, voice AI, calendars, conversations, opportunities, payments, and related resources.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Evidence gathered indicates an official HighLevel MCP endpoint at https://services.leadconnectorhq.com/mcp/ using Bearer authentication and account context headers.

raw_result.blocker: The originally provided developer docs URL appeared stale or broken during browser inspection, so toolkit buildability is supported by secondary official pages already gathered but should be verified against the current canonical developer portal.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Evidence gathered indicates standard OAuth public APIs are limited to 100 requests per 10 seconds per resource with a daily cap of 200,000 requests, and some product-specific APIs have different limits.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official evidence gathered indicates a modern API program with OAuth 2.0, private integration tokens, SDK support, versioned APIs, and an official MCP endpoint for AI-agent access.
raw_result.human_review_reason: The current pass relied on evidence summaries from official-site discovery after the provided Stoplight hint appeared stale, rather than directly reading stable canonical content pages for every structured field. Access tier, exact auth coverage, MCP page canonical URL, and buildability should be revalidated on the live first-party docs/pricing pages.
